title: The Pez Dispenser

jokes.text: Women put on perfume in an interesting way. Ever notice that, guys? They have their little key, Stratego little areas.
jokes.setup: Jerry opens with observational premise about women's perfume application
jokes.punchline: The absurd comparison to board game strategy ('Stratego little areas')
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor with unexpected board game reference. Sets tone for episode.

jokes.text: Women are convinced that this is the most action-packed area that could ever happen in the dating world. What is that, ladies? What is happening here?
jokes.punchline: 'Action-packed area' - treating wrist like an adventure zone
jokes.explanation: Jerry's signature 'What's the deal with...' style executed well

jokes.text: Is that in case you slap the guy or something? He still finds you intriguing? 'Oh, Chanel!'
jokes.setup: Question about the wrist perfume strategy
jokes.punchline: Absurd scenario of aromatic slapping followed by effete 'Oh, Chanel!'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect voice work on 'Oh, Chanel!' - quintessential Jerry

jokes.text: She's a pianist, a classical pianist! She plays the piano!
jokes.punchline: Redundant over-explanation reveals his insecurity
jokes.explanation: Perfect George neurosis - the redundancy shows his desperation to impress

jokes.text: She's a brilliant woman! I sat in her living room, she played the Waldstein Sonata. The Waldstein!
jokes.punchline: Over-emphasis on 'The Waldstein!' as if it's incredibly impressive
jokes.explanation: George's pretentious name-dropping with theatrical delivery is peak character comedy

jokes.text: We did a crossword puzzle together in bed. It was the most fun I ever had in my entire life! Do you hear me? In my life!
jokes.punchline: Crossword puzzle being his sexual peak, delivered with manic intensity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - only George would find crosswords more exciting than sex

jokes.text: You know? Were you talking? I couldn't hear anything.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's complete indifference delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation - Jerry's oblivious response to George's intensity

jokes.text: I was telling you about Noel. Oh, Noel, yeah. The one that plays the bongos.
jokes.setup: George correcting Jerry about his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Jerry's complete misremembering (piano vs bongos)
jokes.explanation: Shows Jerry's self-absorbed nature - complete disregard for George's details

jokes.text: So sidesplittingly funny.
jokes.setup: Jerry's dismissive comment about the bongos
jokes.punchline: George's sarcastic response with formal vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic comeback with George's formal tone

jokes.text: I'm very uncomfortable. I have no power. Why should she have the upper hand? For once, I'd like the upper hand. I have no hand, no hand at all.
jokes.punchline: Escalating obsession with 'hand' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Introduction of 'the hand' concept - becomes central theme of episode

jokes.text: She has the hand. I have no hand. Hand me that, would you? Yeah.
jokes.punchline: Immediate literal 'hand me that' creates accidental pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay moment - accidental pun on George's obsession

jokes.text: How do I get the hand? We all want the hand. Hand is tough to get. You gotta get the hand right from the opening.
jokes.punchline: Treating 'the hand' like a technical skill or game strategy
jokes.explanation: Jerry embracing George's neurosis and adding strategic elements

jokes.text: She'll observe me as I really am, as myself. Maybe I can get some hand that way.
jokes.setup: George planning to show his 'real self'
jokes.punchline: Irony that his 'real self' is manipulative, plus hand callback
jokes.explanation: George's self-awareness is itself manipulative - perfect character consistency

jokes.text: Hey, smell my arm.
jokes.punchline: Immediately demands arm-smelling with no context
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer entrance - maximum weird with minimal words

jokes.text: What? Smell it. Smell it. With all due respect, I don't think so.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's polite but firm refusal with formal language
jokes.explanation: Jerry's diplomatic rejection of absurd request shows his character perfectly

jokes.text: The beach. The beach? Did you go swimming? It's 29 degrees. No, I just joined the Polar Bear Club.
jokes.setup: Beach smell explanation when it's freezing
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of joining extreme winter swimming club
jokes.explanation: Typical Kramer - extreme activity treated as casual hobby

jokes.text: What the hell's a Polar Bear? These people go swimming in the winter. They're terrific.
jokes.punchline: Kramer's enthusiastic endorsement of people who swim in freezing water
jokes.explanation: Kramer's simple enthusiasm for extreme behavior is perfectly in character

jokes.text: It's invigorating. Yeah... so is shock therapy.
jokes.setup: Kramer praising cold swimming as invigorating
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dark comparison to electroshock therapy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor comeback - perfect Jerry response to Kramer's enthusiasm

jokes.text: What is that, a PEZ dispenser? Yeah. You want one?
jokes.punchline: Kramer casually offering random PEZ dispensers
jokes.explanation: Setup for major plot element - PEZ dispenser introduction

jokes.text: Hey, what goes on there, exactly? You don't know? No, I know. I know. You think they have fleas there, don't you? No. Yes, you do, Biff.
jokes.punchline: Kramer's condescending 'Biff' and insistence George thinks literally about fleas
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Kramer being patronizing while George is actually right

jokes.text: You've never been, and you think they have fleas there. All right, I think they have fleas there. So what?
jokes.punchline: George's defensive admission and 'So what?' attitude
jokes.explanation: George's defensive-aggressive response is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Here, you want this? You don't want it? No, I bought five of them. Wow, great. Thanks.
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation he bought five identical PEZ dispensers
jokes.explanation: Typical Kramer excess - buying multiples of random items

jokes.text: I don't know how anyone does this. This must be so nerve-racking. How do they warm up their fingers?
jokes.punchline: Focusing on practical finger-warming logistics
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observational humor - fixating on mundane details

jokes.text: They have a piano backstage they warm up on. No. We would have heard it. You think they just crack their knuckles and come out?
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dismissal and absurd alternative of just knuckle-cracking
jokes.explanation: Sets up later payoff when Noel says she just cracks her knuckles

jokes.text: Don't applaud when she stops playing the first time. It's not over yet. I really resent that. That's directed at me, isn't it?
jokes.punchline: Jerry's immediate paranoid assumption it's a personal attack
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jerry's narcissistic paranoia about general advice

jokes.text: Is this okay? Can I do this?
jokes.setup: Jerry placing PEZ dispenser on Elaine's leg during recital
jokes.punchline: His whispered uncertainty about the appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Setup for the central comedic disaster - Jerry's impulsive mischief

jokes.text: Oh, God. Something I said?
jokes.setup: Elaine has burst into laughter during piano recital
jokes.punchline: Jerry's oblivious question when he caused it
jokes.explanation: Jerry's false innocence when he directly caused the disruption

jokes.text: It's John Mollica. Oh. Oh, John. Oh, hi, John. Hi. What are you doing out here? I was just at this recital and Jerry put this PEZ dispenser on my leg and I just started laughing.
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of the PEZ dispenser explanation
jokes.explanation: The specificity of the PEZ explanation makes it funnier than generic disruption

jokes.text: Really? Boy... you really look great. Oh, thank you.
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate timing and Elaine's polite response
jokes.explanation: Mild awkwardness but not particularly memorable or clever

jokes.text: He's kind of messed up on drugs. I don't know what to do. Have you thought about an intervention?
jokes.setup: John expressing concern about friend on drugs
jokes.punchline: Elaine's casual suggestion of trendy intervention solution
jokes.explanation: Sets up intervention subplot but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: You get all his friends in a room and they confront him to try to get him into rehab. It's a very popular thing now.
jokes.punchline: Describing serious addiction treatment as trendy ('very popular thing now')
jokes.explanation: Treating serious intervention like a lifestyle trend is subtly funny

jokes.text: I'm sorry, George. I'm sorry. What did you put the PEZ dispenser on her leg for? I don't know. It was an impulse. What kind of a sick impulse is that?
jokes.setup: Jerry apologizing for disrupting the recital
jokes.punchline: George's outrage at the 'sick impulse' of PEZ placement
jokes.explanation: George treating PEZ placement as perverted behavior is perfectly neurotic

jokes.text: I'll tell her I was the one who laughed. No, don't say a word. If she thinks my friends are jerks, then I'm a jerk.
jokes.punchline: George's paranoid logic about guilt by association
jokes.explanation: George's narcissistic concern about appearing jerk-adjacent is spot-on

jokes.text: What are you, a baby? All right, tell him. When I was outside, I ran into John Mollica. Really? John Mollica?
jokes.setup: George calling Kramer a baby for being distracted
jokes.punchline: Jerry's immediate excited interest in John Mollica
jokes.explanation: Shows the group's ADD-like attention to different topics

jokes.text: Could we cut to the chase? Cut to the chase? What are you, Joe Hollywood?
jokes.setup: George using 'cut to the chase' expression
jokes.punchline: Jerry's 'Joe Hollywood' mockery of the cliché
jokes.explanation: 'Joe Hollywood' is a perfect Jerry-esque putdown for cliché usage

jokes.text: A lot of people say it. I would lose that. What's that? Lose that? That's not a Hollywood expression. Yes, it is.
jokes.punchline: Jerry using another Hollywood expression while denying it
jokes.explanation: Jerry's hypocrisy in using 'lose that' while criticizing Hollywood speak

jokes.text: We got a situation over here!
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dramatic announcement about the PEZ dispenser loading difficulty
jokes.explanation: Treating PEZ loading like an emergency situation is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: These things are really hard to load.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact complaint about PEZ engineering
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor that becomes important later in episode

jokes.text: You play a hell of a piano. Yeah, I was really moved. Really moved.
jokes.setup: Trying to compliment after the disruption
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top compliments from people who ruined her performance
jokes.explanation: The awkwardness of complimenting someone you just humiliated

jokes.text: PEZ? No, thank you.
jokes.setup: Jerry offering PEZ to the pianist he just disrupted
jokes.punchline: Noel's polite refusal of the source of her humiliation
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - offering the weapon of disruption as peace offering

jokes.text: Anyone who would laugh at a recital is probably some sort of lunatic, anyway. I mean, only a sick, twisted mind could be that rude and ignorant.
jokes.punchline: Elaine sitting right there being described as lunatic with sick, twisted mind
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony - condemning someone who's sitting right there

jokes.text: Well, you know, maybe some mental defective put something stupid on her leg.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to deflect blame from Elaine
jokes.punchline: Jerry calling himself a mental defective while defending his action
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-deprecation while simultaneously making excuses - very Jerry

jokes.text: Even if this so-called mental defective did put something on her leg, she's still the one who laughed.
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on blaming the victim while insulting Jerry
jokes.explanation: Noel's rigid inability to see any perspective but her own

jokes.text: I'll never forget that laugh for the rest of my life.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overstatement about remembering Elaine's laugh forever
jokes.explanation: The melodrama of trauma from someone laughing at classical music

jokes.text: I'm sure she would apologize if she could. Probably someone is holding her back, maybe against every fibre of her being.
jokes.punchline: Elaine's dramatic 'fibre of her being' metaphor for being prevented from apologizing
jokes.explanation: Elaine's overwrought language while actually wanting to apologize creates perfect irony

jokes.text: If she wants to continue to have a fibre of her being, she'll be very careful.
jokes.punchline: Noel's ominous threat using same metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with escalation to actual threat - perfect comedy timing

jokes.text: By the way, how do you warm up your fingers before you play? I just crack my knuckles.
jokes.punchline: Noel's casual answer proves Jerry right from earlier discussion
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to earlier setup - Jerry was right about the knuckle cracking

jokes.text: I said, I don't feel like it. All right... I'll... I'll call you. I'll call you, and we'll talk on the phone. Have a telephone communiqué.
jokes.punchline: George's increasingly formal language culminating in 'telephone communiqué'
jokes.explanation: 'Telephone communiqué' is perfectly pretentious George vocabulary when nervous

jokes.text: So, fine. Everything's fine. Everything good? Good. I feel good. Fine. That's fine. That's good. Okay. Okey-doke.
jokes.setup: George trying to play off his rejection
jokes.punchline: Nervous rambling with repetitive 'fine/good' mantras
jokes.explanation: Classic George meltdown - the repetitive affirmations show his complete panic

jokes.text: You know, I think Kramer might have been indirectly responsible for getting Richie involved in drugs. What? How?
jokes.setup: Jerry making connection between Kramer and drug addiction
jokes.punchline: The absurd premise that Kramer caused someone's drug problem
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the Gatorade story - blaming Kramer for addiction is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: So Kramer says to Richie: 'Why don't you dump the bucket of Gatorade on Marty Benson's head?' The club owner. Yeah. So Richie goes ahead and does it.
jokes.punchline: The innocent Gatorade dump suggestion
jokes.explanation: Sets up the dark turn - seems innocent at first

jokes.text: The guy was, like, 67 years old. It was freezing out. He caught a cold, developed pneumonia, a month later he was dead. Shut up.
jokes.punchline: Escalation from innocent celebration to actual death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation - innocent sports tradition becomes manslaughter

jokes.text: All the comedians were happy because he's one of these club owners nobody liked. But Richie was never the same. What about Kramer? He's the same.
jokes.setup: Consequences of the club owner's death
jokes.punchline: Kramer being completely unaffected while Richie is traumatized
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - Kramer's obliviousness to consequences he causes

jokes.text: I don't have a good apartment for an intervention. The furniture, it's very nonconfrontational.
jokes.punchline: Treating furniture style as relevant to addiction confrontation
jokes.explanation: Genius observational humor - 'nonconfrontational furniture' is perfectly Jerry

jokes.text: Oh, sure. The guy I told to pour the Gatorade that killed Marty Benson?
jokes.punchline: Kramer's casual reference to his role in the death
jokes.explanation: Kramer's matter-of-fact reference to causing someone's death is darkly hilarious

jokes.text: Can I get in on that? What do you think, it's like a poker game?
jokes.punchline: Jerry's comparison to casual poker game
jokes.explanation: Good comparison treating serious intervention like casual entertainment

jokes.text: No, intervene, go ahead. Intervene all you want. I'm just afraid you might be interfering while we're intervening.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's wordplay on intervene vs interfere
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with intervene/interfere while highlighting Kramer's disruptive nature

jokes.text: Stop smelling your arm!
jokes.punchline: George's exasperated command
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier arm-smelling, shows everyone's fed up

jokes.text: You know, I got a great idea for a cologne. It's called 'The Beach.' You spray it on, and it's just like you came home from the beach.
jokes.punchline: The completely literal concept of beach-scented cologne
jokes.explanation: Typical Kramer scheme - taking literal experience and making it commercial

jokes.text: I can't believe I'm saying this, but that's not a bad idea.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's reluctant admission that the crazy idea might work
jokes.explanation: Jerry's surprise at finding merit in Kramer's absurd idea

jokes.text: It's over. It's definitely over. She broke up with you? No, but she's going to. I can sense it.
jokes.punchline: Pre-emptive breakup assumption based on 'sensing' it
jokes.explanation: Perfect George neurosis - assuming the worst before it happens

jokes.text: I'm very good at going in reverse in my car. Why isn't Postum a more popular drink?
jokes.setup: George reading his conversation topics list
jokes.punchline: Completely random and boring conversation starters
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly mundane conversation topics reveal George's social incompetence

jokes.text: Yeah, Postum is underrated.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sincere agreement about obscure drink
jokes.explanation: Jerry's matter-of-fact agreement with George's random observation

jokes.text: You know what that means. What's wrong with lunch? Lunch is fine at the beginning, then you move on to dinner. You don't go back to lunch. It's like being demoted.
jokes.setup: Noel suggesting lunch instead of dinner
jokes.punchline: George's elaborate theory of meal hierarchy and relationship demotion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dating theory - treating meal choice as relationship thermometer

jokes.text: I'll never do a crossword puzzle with her again, I know it. I like the jumble. You ever do the jumble?
jokes.punchline: Jerry's complete non-sequitur about preferring jumbles
jokes.explanation: Jerry's typical self-absorption - missing George's emotional point

jokes.text: I have no power, you understand? I need hand! I have no hand! Why don't you break up with her. What? Break up with her. You reverse everything that way. A preemptive breakup.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's strategic 'preemptive breakup' solution
jokes.explanation: 'Preemptive breakup' is genius relationship strategy that only Jerry would suggest

jokes.text: This is an incredible idea. Yeah. I got nothing to lose. We either break up, which she would do anyway, but I go out with some dignity, or I completely turn the tables!
jokes.punchline: His excitement about getting dignity from fake breakup manipulation
jokes.explanation: George finding 'dignity' in manipulation is perfectly backward George logic

jokes.text: So I'm afraid I am gonna have to break up with you. You're breaking up with me? Yes. I am breaking up with you.
jokes.punchline: Noel's shock at being dumped when she was going to dump him
jokes.explanation: The plan working perfectly - Noel's genuine surprise at being dumped

jokes.text: Shocked? Never expected this, did you? I thought everything was fine. Well, live and learn.
jokes.punchline: His smug 'live and learn' while being completely dishonest
jokes.explanation: George's condescending wisdom while executing deception is peak character comedy

jokes.text: I didn't mean to hurt you, kid. Now, stop it, please. What do you want? I know I can make you happy.
jokes.punchline: Noel's desperate pleading and promise to make him happy
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal - now she's begging him not to leave

jokes.text: When you're playing the piano, do you think about me? I don't know. This is what I'm talking about. Okay, I'll think about you. All the time? All the time? You know... Okay, all the time. I can't hear you. All the time!
jokes.punchline: Making her promise to think about him constantly while playing piano
jokes.explanation: Brilliant power play - George finally getting the control he wanted

jokes.text: See? It's not so hard.
jokes.setup: Getting Noel to submit to his demands
jokes.punchline: George's satisfied observation about control being 'not so hard'
jokes.explanation: George's casual satisfaction with manipulation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: That is the dumbest idea I've ever heard. Whoa, wait a minute. You... Did you hear what I just said? You think people are gonna pay $80 a bottle to smell like dead fish and seaweed?
jokes.punchline: Brutal reality check about beach smell being 'dead fish and seaweed'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Kramer's romantic beach vision with harsh reality

jokes.text: That's why people take showers when they come home from the beach. It's an objectionable, offensive odour.
jokes.punchline: Pointing out people shower to remove beach smell, not preserve it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical takedown - people actively try to remove beach smell

jokes.text: So you don't think that it's a good idea?
jokes.setup: Steve's brutal rejection of cologne idea
jokes.punchline: Kramer's oblivious question after devastating critique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer - completely missing obvious social cues and rejection

jokes.text: The membranes get dry, and it just starts bleeding. Since I was a kid, so I have to stick a tissue up there. You have to work like that? Yeah. Nobody minds. Nobody's ever said anything to me.
jokes.setup: John explaining his bloody nose tissue situation
jokes.punchline: His oblivious belief that nobody minds his tissue-in-nose appearance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor - John's complete lack of self-awareness about appearance

jokes.text: I looked. I don't see any ice cubes. Well, then I guess there's no ice cubes. I can't drink this. It's warm.
jokes.setup: John complaining about lack of ice
jokes.punchline: Jerry's matter-of-fact response and John's continued complaining
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment - Jerry's bluntness with demanding guest

jokes.text: He can't stay. This is for close friends only. Hey, I'm a friend. Who told him to pour the Gatorade over Marty Benson's head?
jokes.setup: John trying to exclude Kramer from intervention
jokes.punchline: Kramer's defense being that he caused the original trauma
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Kramer using his role in causing trauma as qualification to help

jokes.text: Hey, I got somebody to make up that cologne for me, bigmouth. Somebody's going to make that crap?
jokes.setup: Kramer revealing someone will make his cologne
jokes.punchline: Steve's continued disbelief and insult
jokes.explanation: Continues the cologne subplot with Steve's disgust

jokes.text: Sure. We used to do that when one of our Polar Bears stopped coming. We'd go to his house: 'What, you don't want to be a Polar Bear anymore? It's too cold for you?'
jokes.setup: Polar Bear explaining their intervention process
jokes.punchline: Confronting dropouts with sarcastic cold tolerance questions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - intervention for quitting winter swimming club

jokes.text: You don't wanna go out with me because I'm a bartender, right? Look, you know, I don't really think that this is appropriate right now. Is it because I have a tissue in my nose?
jokes.setup: John hitting on Elaine during intervention setup
jokes.punchline: His oblivious focus on tissue-in-nose as the dating obstacle
jokes.explanation: Good cringe timing - inappropriate romantic advance with nose tissue concern

jokes.text: Jerry, let me tell you something. A man without hand is not a man. I've got so much hand, I'm coming out of my gloves.
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top metaphor about overflowing hand power
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of hand metaphor with George's typical over-the-top victory lap

jokes.text: Even if I were dragged through manure, I still wouldn't put that stuff on. This man is a genius! A genius!
jokes.setup: Steve's continued cologne disgust vs George's Kramer praise
jokes.punchline: Extreme contrast between manure-dragging refusal and genius declaration
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between two extreme reactions to same person

jokes.text: The male kangaroo doesn't have a pouch. Only the female has it, so the male has pouch envy! Why should she have this huge pouch and I have nothing? I have things to carry too. At least give me a pocket.
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing male kangaroo jealousy with specific complaints
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist observation - kangaroo gender politics with human psychology

jokes.text: That laugh. That's the laugh. That's it! You're the one! No, no. No, it was an accident. It wasn't my fault! It was Jerry!
jokes.setup: Noel recognizing Elaine's laugh from recital
jokes.punchline: Elaine's panicked blame-shifting to Jerry
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as the secret is revealed and Elaine panics

jokes.text: You put a PEZ dispenser on her leg during my recital? I didn't know she would laugh. And, you... You lied to me, George. You lied to me!
jokes.punchline: Noel's outrage at both the prank and George's deception
jokes.explanation: Good payoff as all the deceptions unravel simultaneously

jokes.text: I am breaking up with you. You can't break up with me. I've got hand! And you're gonna need it.
jokes.punchline: George's desperate hand claim and Noel's threatening response
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal - George's hand victory immediately stripped away with threatening pun

jokes.text: The PEZ dispenser. He picks it up, he's staring at it. It's like he's hypnotized by it. Then he's telling us this story about how when he was a kid, he was in the car with his father and his father was trying to load one of them.
jokes.punchline: PEZ dispenser triggering childhood trauma memory
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the big PEZ story payoff - childhood trauma connection

jokes.text: So as the father's trying to load it, he loses control of the car, crashes into a high school cafeteria. Get out. Nobody's hurt, but PEZ was all over the car. And the dispenser was destroyed, virtually beyond recognition.
jokes.punchline: Escalation to cafeteria crash with PEZ destruction detail
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - PEZ loading difficulty becomes major trauma with perfect absurd details

jokes.text: So as he's telling the story, he starts crying. What did you do? What do you think? I gave him my PEZ dispenser.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's matter-of-fact solution of giving him the PEZ dispenser
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry solution - practical gesture that accidentally solves the whole problem

jokes.text: Two hours later, he checks into Smither's clinic. Talked to the doctor yesterday. He's doing great on the rehab, he's hooked on PEZ.
jokes.punchline: Trading drug addiction for PEZ addiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending - solving addiction by creating new candy addiction

jokes.text: He's eating them like there's no tomorrow. What's a three-letter word for candy?
jokes.punchline: George's oblivious crossword question during serious conversation
jokes.explanation: George's self-absorbed focus on crossword puzzle during friend's addiction story

jokes.text: Jawbreakers, to me, was like the ultimate challenge candy. It was like candy manufacturers were sitting in their laboratories, going: 'I wonder if they'll eat this. This seems pretty tough to eat. Let's market it as some sort of experiment.'
jokes.punchline: Imagining candy makers as sadistic experimenters
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observational humor - treating candy marketing as conspiracy against consumers

jokes.text: But the concept of Jawbreakers... What was the concept? Multicoloured cement balls for a quarter? Was that the idea behind it? 'Let's see if we can hurt them. Let's see if they'll pay to be hurt.'
jokes.punchline: Describing candy as 'cement balls' and masochistic consumer transaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - 'pay to be hurt' perfectly captures absurdity of difficult candy

jokes.text: I did it, I ate them, I loved them. 'Oh, this is very painful. I'm really hurting. And this is hurting me a lot. I love them.'
jokes.punchline: Self-aware masochistic internal monologue about enjoying pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-aware conclusion - Jerry acknowledging his own masochistic candy consumption

tmdb_overview: Kramer's advice gives George 'the upper hand' in his relationship with a pianist, whose recital is ruined by Elaine's outburst of laughter.
